creative_brief: Clean, minimal layout with a single-column design. Use a simple supply chain diagram (farm → hub → customer vs. farm → broker → warehouse → distributor → store → customer) as the hero visual to reinforce the middleman argument. Muted earth tones, one green CTA button.
subject: The grocery industry doesn't have a margin problem
preheader: It has a middleman problem. And that's exactly where the opportunity sits.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Last week a friend asked me why I'd build a grocery business. "Margins are terrible," he said. "Everyone knows that."

He's not wrong — if you're picturing the model most people picture. A chain store paying warehouse fees, distributor cuts, broker margins, and slotting fees before a single apple hits the shelf.

But here's what most investors miss. The margin isn't thin because food is cheap. It's thin because the supply chain is bloated.

Strip out the unnecessary middlemen — the brokers, the warehouses, the 2,400km of cold chain — and something shifts. The farmer gets paid more. The customer pays less. And the business in the middle keeps what used to disappear into logistics.

That's FarmThru.

We work with around 15–20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW. Customers order online, then collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. No warehouse network. No shelf fees. No lock-in subscriptions — just order when you want.

It's a hub-and-collect model built to do one thing well: remove everything between the farm and your family.

I won't pretend this is simple. Building a new kind of grocery store means earning trust — from farmers, from families, from people like you who look at the numbers and ask hard questions.

But we believe the thesis is sound: when you cut the waste out of the supply chain, everyone wins. Including the business.

We're preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign so everyday Australians can own a piece of what we're building. If you want first access, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A before we go live — you can join the VIP waitlist today for a $5 refundable deposit.

No commitment beyond that. Just a front-row seat.

→ Join the VIP waitlist

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
